heading: Definitions
label: 2266 Definitions
notes: Amendments2018—Pars. (11) to (13). added pars. (11) to (13).Pub. L. 115–3342006—Par. (5). , added par. (5) and struck out heading and text of former par. (5). Text read as follows: “The term ‘protection order’ includes any injunction or other order issued for the purpose of preventing violent or threatening acts or harassment against, or contact or communication with or physical proximity to, another person, including any temporary or final order issued by a civil and criminal court (other than a support or child custody order issued pursuant to State divorce and child custody laws, except to the extent that such an order is entitled to full faith and credit under other Federal law) whether obtained by filing an independent action or as a pendente lite order in another proceeding so long as any civil order was issued in response to a complaint, petition, or motion filed by or on behalf of a person seeking protection.”Pub. L. 109–162, § 106(d)(1)Par. (7)(A). , which directed amendment of cls. (i) and (ii) by substituting “2261A—Pub. L. 109–162, § 106(d)(2)“(I) a spouse or former spouse of the abuser, a person who shares a child in common with the abuser, and a person who cohabits or has cohabited as a spouse with the abuser; or“(II) a person who is or has been in a social relationship of a romantic or intimate nature with the abuser, as determined by the length of the relationship, the type of relationship, and the frequency of interaction between the persons involved in the relationship”for “2261A, a spouse or former spouse of the abuser, a person who shares a child in common with the abuser, and a person who cohabits or has cohabited as a spouse with the abuser”, was executed only to cl. (i) to reflect the probable intent of Congress because the quoted language to be deleted does not appear in cl. (ii).Par. (7)(A)(ii). , added cl. (ii) and struck out former cl. (ii) which read as follows: “section 2261A, a spouse or former spouse of the target of the stalking, a person who shares a child in common with the target of the stalking, and a person who cohabits or has cohabited as a spouse with the target of the stalking; and”.Pub. L. 109–271, § 2(c)Par. (10). , substituted “. The existence of such a relationship is” for “and the existence of such a relationship” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 109–271, § 2(i), added par. (10).Pub. L. 109–162, § 116(b)2000— reenacted section catchline without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text defined “bodily injury”, “Indian country”, “protection order”, “spouse or intimate partner”, “State”, and “travel across State lines”.Pub. L. 106–386

children.heading: .—Bodily injury
children.label: 1 .—Bodily injury
children.text: The term “bodily injury” means any act, except one done in self-defense, that results in physical injury or sexual abuse.

children.heading: .—Course of conduct
children.label: 2 .—Course of conduct
children.text: The term “course of conduct” means a pattern of conduct composed of 2 or more acts, evidencing a continuity of purpose.

children.heading: .—Enter or leave indian country
children.label: 3 .—Enter or leave indian country
children.text: The term “enter or leave Indian country” includes leaving the jurisdiction of 1 tribal government and entering the jurisdiction of another tribal government.

children.heading: .—Indian country
children.label: 4 .—Indian country
children.text: The term “Indian country” has the meaning stated in .section 1151 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any injunction, restraining order, or any other order issued by a civil or criminal court for the purpose of preventing violent or threatening acts or harassment against, sexual violence, or contact or communication with or physical proximity to, another person, including any temporary or final order issued by a civil or criminal court whether obtained by filing an independent action or as a pendente lite order in another proceeding so long as any civil or criminal order was issued in response to a complaint, petition, or motion filed by or on behalf of a person seeking protection; and

children.label: B
children.text: any support, child custody or visitation provisions, orders, remedies or relief issued as part of a protection order, restraining order, or injunction pursuant to State, tribal, territorial, or local law authorizing the issuance of protection orders, restraining orders, or injunctions for the protection of victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, dating violence, or stalking.

children.heading: .—Serious bodily injury
children.label: 6 .—Serious bodily injury
children.text: The term “serious bodily injury” has the meaning stated in section 2119(2).

children.label: I
children.text: a spouse or former spouse of the abuser, a person who shares a child in common with the abuser, and a person who cohabits or has cohabited as a spouse with the abuser; or

children.label: II
children.text: a person who is or has been in a social relationship of a romantic or intimate nature with the abuser, as determined by the length of the relationship, the type of relationship, and the frequency of interaction between the persons involved in the relationship; and

children.label: I
children.text: a spouse or former spouse of the target of the stalking, a person who shares a child in common with the target of the stalking, and a person who cohabits or has cohabited as a spouse with the target of the stalking; or

children.label: II
children.text: a person who is or has been in a social relationship of a romantic or intimate nature with the target of the stalking, as determined by the length of the relationship, the type of the relationship, and the frequency of interaction between the persons involved in the relationship.1So in original. The period probably should be “; and”.1

children.label: B
children.text: any other person similarly situated to a spouse who is protected by the domestic or family violence laws of the State or tribal jurisdiction in which the injury occurred or where the victim resides.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 8 .—State
children.text: The term “State” includes a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and a commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.

children.heading: .—Travel in interstate or foreign commerce
children.label: 9 .—Travel in interstate or foreign commerce
children.text: The term “travel in interstate or foreign commerce” does not include travel from 1 State to another by an individual who is a member of an Indian tribe and who remains at all times in the territory of the Indian tribe of which the individual is a member.

children.label: A
children.text: the length of the relationship; and

children.label: B
children.text: the type of relationship; and

children.label: C
children.text: the frequency of interaction between the persons involved in the relationship.

children.heading: .—Pet
children.label: 11 .—Pet
children.text: The term “pet” means a domesticated animal, such as a dog, cat, bird, rodent, fish, turtle, or other animal that is kept for pleasure rather than for commercial purposes.

children.heading: .—Emotional support animal
children.label: 12 .—Emotional support animal
children.text: The term “emotional support animal” means an animal that is covered by the exclusion specified in section 5.303 of title 24, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation), and that is not a service animal.

children.heading: .—Service animal
children.label: 13 .—Service animal
children.text: The term “service animal” has the meaning given the term in section 36.104 of title 28, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation).
